name: Alten S.A.
lede: Alten runs four capability centers across four Indian cities. Bengaluru, Chennai, Hyderabad and Pune, covering gcc/gic, built over sixteen years.
insight: Search elsewhere and you will usually find one Alten office in India. We map four, across four cities, each tagged by center type and function. Knowing the whole footprint is what turns a name on a list into an account you can plan against.
metaTitle: Alten S.A. GCC in India: Centers, Footprint & Leaders | Bamboo Reports
metaDescription: Alten runs 4 verified GCC centers across 4 Indian cities (Bengaluru, Chennai, Hyderabad, Pune), covering gcc/gic. See the full India footprint, org map and contacts on Bamboo Reports.

leaders.departments: Finance, IT, HR, GCC leadership and Procurement

titles.title: Senior Director & Country Head, Talent Acquisition

about.description: Alten's India footprint covers Bengaluru, Chennai, Hyderabad and Pune, spanning gcc/gic. The centers deliver IT, R&D and ER&D and Procurement for the group's global operations.
about.industry: IT, System Integration and Consulting Services, Corporate Services

bandCopy: Exact center locations, per-center headcount, the technology stack and all thirteen decision-makers, in one profile.

faq.q: How many GCC centers does Alten operate in India?
faq.a: Alten operates 4 active Global Capability Center sites in India.

faq.q: Which Indian cities does Alten have GCC centers in?
faq.a: Alten's India GCC footprint spans Bengaluru, Chennai, Hyderabad and Pune.

faq.q: What types of GCC centers does Alten run in India?
faq.a: Alten runs gcc/gic centers in India.

faq.q: How many people work at Alten's India GCCs?
faq.a: Alten's India centers employ in the 1,000 to 5,000 range. The exact aggregate and the headcount at each site are in the full profile.

faq.q: Who leads Alten's GCC in India?
faq.a: Bamboo Reports tracks 13 leaders at Alten's India centers, across Finance, IT, HR, GCC leadership and Procurement. Names and contact details open with a free account.

sources: company filings, LinkedIn, NASSCOM and Bamboo Reports' primary research
